title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

notes: I guess gold is the correct choice, the game works great but it won't go into fullscreen.  If you switch to fullscreen it drops to the toolbar and won't come back up.  Other than that it runs perfect as far as I can tell.  However this issue keeps it from being Platinum (I think some people need to understand that platinum means it's perfect out of the box).
title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

notes: One of my PC's this works out of the box so I would almost give it a Platinum until I tried it on my second machine. My second machine experienced the screen stuck in the taskbar. The only workaround I found for that is to change the resolution down, back up to what I want, and then change to fullscreen. Otherwise it gets stuck in the window menu.
title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom
Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

notes: Everything works out of the box, performance was good on high graphical settings, no glitches at all.
title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

notes: Runs great, just not in fullscreen mode, must change to borderless window to get the game to run.  After playing a while I started to notice some artifacts, not game breaking  Restarting the application fixes it temporarily.
title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

notes:
The game runs well on my Dualshock 4 controller (it even has correct button icons), the frame rate is good (stays at 60 FPS most of the time on 1440p and high settings) and there are no issues with graphics most of the time. The only issue is the occasional graphical bugs, where some of the characters' 3D models will start distorting, requiring the game to be restarted before stopping: https://steamuserimages-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/947341929947080143/6F0B48FB1B5942B65F2D059D547C46CDF7E45004/

I haven't seen this issue reported anywhere else, so it might be a regression in the latest upstream Mesa drivers, or an hardware issue (I hope not). Your mileage may vary.
title: Ni no Kuni™ II: Revenant Kingdom

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 With Max-Q Design
notes: Runs fine if I unplug my mouse and plug a controller on in, disabling the touchpad. Odd sensitivity to mouse input...
